scan.code: mp-api / Materials Project (a DATABASE, not an engine)
scan.source_root: AtomisticSkills/.agents/skills/mat-db-mp/ (query_mp.py, get_elasticity.py, get_magnetism.py, get_structure_by_id.py, find_similar_structures.py), plus mat-stability/query_mp_hull.py, mat-electronic-structure/get_mp_electronic_structure.py, mat-phonon/get_mp_phonon.py, mat-phase-diagram/get_phase_diagram.py, mat-elemental-energies/query_elements.py, mat-structure-novelty/match_structure.py, mat-ionic-substitution/find_structures_for_composition.py
scan.entry_count: 18 entry objects. Original scanner statuses were 9 already-mapped / 7 new-node-candidate / 2 representation-only, computed against an EARLIER graph snapshot. DEEP REVIEW (2026-07-09) re-checked every status against the CURRENT docs/data/graph.json (66 nodes) and found FormationEnergy, EnergyAboveHull, YoungsModulus, PoissonRatio, MagneticMoment, Voltage, SurfaceEnergy ALL PRESENT (they landed after the scanner ran, and committed instance records already reference them). Corrected live counts: 14 already-mapped, 2 new-node-candidate (mp-band-gap, mp-mass-density; universal_anisotropy still tagless), 2 representation-only. Per-entry status fields updated in place; see the 'Review verdicts (2026-07-09)' section of the .md for the reclassification record.
scan.code_versions: mp-api 0.41.2, emmet-core 0.85.1 (both importable in miniconda base: /Users/juicy/miniconda3/lib/python3.12/site-packages/{mp_api/client, emmet/core}). Document models read directly from emmet/core/summary.py, elasticity.py, magnetism.py, electrode.py, structure.py, thermo.py, types/enums.py. MPRester routes verified present in mp_api/client/mprester.py: summary, thermo, elasticity, magnetism, phonon, electronic_structure_bandstructure, electronic_structure_dos, tasks, insertion_electrodes. MP-served example docs elsewhere in the repo carry pymatgen_version 2024.11.13.
scan.role_in_software: Materials Project is NOT an engine; it is a DATABASE, i.e. a representation whose artifacts are database RECORDS carrying already-computed values for mapped quantities. Those values were themselves computed by VASP DFT workflows (MP's provenance is the atomate2/VASP stack the atomate2-vasp scan catalogs), so the VASP catalog's conventions apply to MP fields: energy is a VASP e_0_energy-family variant with MP2020 corrections, stress (in the trajectory endpoint) is kbar, and elastic/magnetic values are the pymatgen post-processing outputs (bulk_modulus.vrh in GPa, total_magnetization in mu_B). AtomisticSkills reaches MP through mp_api.client.MPRester (mpr.materials.<endpoint>.search / .get_*_by_id). MP fills two distinct roles for the map: (a) a RETRIEVAL representation of quantities the map already has (Structure, ElasticConstants/BulkModulus/ShearModulus, ForceConstants, Frequency/PhononDOS, and per-atom energetics), and (b) an INSTANCE SOURCE: each (material_id, value) record is a piece of evidence (kind 'simulation', ref 'materials-project' + mp-id) with the MP functional / mixing-scheme as conditions provenance.
scan.canonical_units_note: The openmaterials map's canonical units (omai/operator/registry.py QUANTITY_TAGS + the QE/VASP/pymatgen scans) are the reference. PRECISION DUTY for MP specifically: MP's elasticity is served in GPa (NOT eV/A^3 like raw pymatgen ElasticTensor), and MP's young_modulus is served in SI Pa (NOT GPa); MP's total_magnetization is TOTAL mu_B (per cell), with separate per-formula-unit (mu_B/f.u.) and per-volume (mu_B/A^3) normalized fields. These normalization/unit differences are the traps this scan exists to catch.
scan.instance_source_design_pointer: See the dedicated 'instance_source_design' object at the end of this file for how MP records enter docs/data/instances/ as evidence (variable, conditions carrying functional/mixing-scheme, source.ref format with mp-id).

entries.name: mp-structure-record
entries.quantity: The crystal structure of an MP material (lowest-energy relaxed structure), as a database record
entries.units: Lattice vectors and cartesian coordinates in ANGSTROM; fractional coordinates dimensionless. SummaryDoc.structure is a pymatgen Structure; mpr.materials.get_structure_by_material_id returns it directly.
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.summary.search(fields=['structure']) -> SummaryDoc.structure; mpr.materials.get_structure_by_material_id(material_id); serialized to CIF via pymatgen CifWriter
entries.conversion_to_canonical: angstrom is the map's length unit; opaque Structure node. Same realization as the pymatgen crystal-structure entry (MP hands back a pymatgen Structure). NO conversion.

source: emmet/core/summary.py:422-426 (SummaryDoc.structure: 'The lowest energy structure for this material.')

maps_to.notes: Direct match to the opaque Structure node. MP is a RETRIEVAL representation: the record carries a relaxed VASP structure. As an instance source, an MP structure is not a scalar 'value' and is not ingested as a docs/data/instances record (the instance store holds scalar physical values); it is provenance/context for the scalar records that share its material_id.

entries.name: mp-cell-volume-and-density
entries.quantity: Unit-cell volume, mass density, and atomic packing density of an MP material

density_atomic.note: A^3 per atom in emmet (volume/num_sites), despite the field description saying atoms per cm^3

entries.units: volume ANGSTROM^3 (StructureMetadata.volume 'Total volume ... in Angstroms^3'); density g/cm^3 (structure.py:67-68 'Density in grams per cm^3'); density_atomic is actually volume/num_sites = A^3/atom in emmet's populator (structure.py:157), contradicting its own 'atoms per cm^3' description string (a known emmet doc/impl mismatch).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: volume A^3 matches CellVolume (QE bohr^3 needs the 0.14818471117057203 factor; MP is already A^3). density g/cm^3 has NO map node (candidate below). nsites -> AtomCount (dimensionless).

usage: mat-db-mp/scripts/query_mp.py:10 ('Structural properties (density, volume, nsites)'), :194 (doc.nsites used in force-count validation)
source: emmet/core/structure.py:61-64 (volume A^3), :67-68 (density g/cm^3), :71-74,155-157 (density_atomic; populated as volume/num_sites = A^3/atom, description string 'atoms per cm^3' is inconsistent with impl)

maps_to.notes: volume -> CellVolume (A^3, direct), nsites -> AtomCount. density (g/cm^3) is a NEW candidate (see mp-mass-density entry). density_atomic in emmet is A^3/atom (impl) not atoms/cm^3 (its description); if ever ingested, trust the impl, not the string.

entries.name: mp-total-energy-and-per-atom
entries.quantity: MP DFT total energy per atom (corrected and uncorrected), and the raw per-cell VASP total energy from the trajectory endpoint, used for stability and MLIP training
entries.units: energy_per_atom and uncorrected_energy_per_atom: eV/ATOM (summary.py:157-165). Trajectory endpoint gives PER-CELL raw VASP energy in eV (e_wo_entrp, 'energy without entropy'), which the skill divides by nsites for eV/atom. THREE distinct energy quantities live here: (1) uncorrected_energy_per_atom = raw DFT eV/atom; (2) energy_per_atom = MP2020-corrected eV/atom; (3) trajectory e_wo_entrp = raw per-cell eV (a DIFFERENT VASP variant than pymatgen final_energy's e_0_energy).
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.summary.search(fields=['energy_per_atom','uncorrected_energy_per_atom','nsites']) -> SummaryDoc; mpr.materials.tasks.get_trajectory(material_id) -> frames with e_wo_entrp / forces / stress (raw VASP, MP2020-uncorrected)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: The map's TotalEnergy node is PER-CELL / extensive (registry tag total_energy 'per simulation cell'; per the pymatgen+VASP scans). So MP's PER-CELL trajectory e_wo_entrp maps to TotalEnergy (eV); but energy_per_atom / uncorrected_energy_per_atom are PER-ATOM and do NOT map to TotalEnergy (candidate FormationEnergy/per-atom-energy nodes, cf. pymatgen scan). VASP TotalEnergy is e_0_energy (VASP scan) whereas the MP trajectory route here harvests e_wo_entrp: a DIFFERENT smearing variant, so a cross-code EXPECTED_AGREE with a QE/VASP TotalEnergy must match the SAME variant. MP2020 CORRECTION TRAP: energy_per_atom (corrected) != uncorrected_energy_per_atom (raw); the skill explicitly uses the raw trajectory e_wo_entrp for MLIP training BECAUSE summary energy_per_atom includes MP2020 anion/+U corrections (query_mp.py:141-142).

usage: mat-db-mp/scripts/query_mp.py:8 ('energy_above_hull, formation_energy_per_atom, energy_per_atom'), :233-241 (fields block: structure, energy_per_atom, uncorrected_energy_per_atom, nsites, energy_above_hull), :260-261 (comment: 'Use trajectory e_wo_entrp (raw VASP energy) instead of summary energy_per_atom which includes MP2020 corrections'), :265 (mpr.materials.tasks.get_trajectory(mid)), :272-277 (frame.get('e_wo_entrp'); traj_energy = e_wo_entrp[-1]), :328 (energy_per_atom = traj_energy / doc.nsites). DEEP-REVIEW ANCHOR FIX: scanner cited :114-127/:141-142/:146-159/:209, off by ~100 lines; the trajectory logic actually lives at 260-328. Stress is served in kbar (:285 comment '3x3 matrix in kbar') and converted by KBAR_TO_EV_A3 = 1/160.21766208 (:203); the physics/units claims are correct, only the line numbers were wrong.
source: emmet/core/summary.py:157-160 (uncorrected_energy_per_atom eV/atom 'total DFT energy per atom'), :162-165 (energy_per_atom eV/atom 'total corrected DFT energy per atom'), :480-482 (thermo field list)
emmet/core/thermo.py:62,68 (uncorrected vs corrected energy_per_atom)

maps_to.notes: Only the PER-CELL trajectory e_wo_entrp maps to TotalEnergy (eV). energy_per_atom / uncorrected_energy_per_atom are per-atom and belong to the same FormationEnergy/EnergyAboveHull family the pymatgen scan flagged for separate nodes. RECORD the MP2020-correction provenance (corrected vs uncorrected) and the e_wo_entrp vs e_0_energy VASP-variant distinction as conditions. As an instance source: energy_per_atom is a cleaner per-material scalar than the per-cell trajectory energy for evidence purposes, but there is no per-atom-TotalEnergy node yet, so ingest it only once FormationEnergy/per-atom nodes land.

entries.name: mp-formation-energy
entries.quantity: MP formation energy per atom relative to elemental reference phases
entries.units: eV/ATOM (summary.py:167-170 'The formation energy per atom in eV/atom.')
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.summary.search(fields=['formation_energy_per_atom']) -> SummaryDoc; also available on the thermo endpoint; filterable via criteria['formation_energy_per_atom'] = (None, max)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV/atom. Matches the map's IN-FLIGHT FormationEnergy candidate exactly: registry tag 'formation_energy' = 'Formation energy per atom relative to elemental reference phases (intensive, eV/atom; distinct from the per-cell total energy)'. MP GROUNDS THIS DIRECTLY with a served field (no in-skill computation needed).

usage: mat-db-mp/scripts/query_mp.py:8,99 (default properties include formation_energy_per_atom), :127-128 (criteria filter formation_energy_max)
source: emmet/core/summary.py:167-170 (formation_energy_per_atom eV/atom), :482 (thermo field list)

maps_to.notes: DEEP-REVIEW STATUS FLIP (new-node-candidate -> already-mapped): the FormationEnergy node IS present in docs/data/graph.json, and a real committed instance already uses it: docs/data/instances/li2o-mp-1960-formationenergy-atomisticskills-mat-phase-diagram-li2o.json (value -2.0616 eV/atom, conditions {thermo: GGA+U, polymorph: ground state, references: Materials Project elemental}, material 'Li2O (mp-1960)'). The registry has the 'formation_energy' tag (eV/atom, intensive). MP grounds it DIRECTLY via SummaryDoc.formation_energy_per_atom (served field, eV/atom). Cleanest MP-as-instance-source scalar. Trap (UNCHANGED): MP's formation energy uses MP-fitted elemental references + MP2020 corrections, distinct from the AtomisticSkills MLIP mat-* formation energies; an EXPECTED_AGREE across the two must account for the reference-state and correction-scheme difference.

entries.name: mp-energy-above-hull
entries.quantity: MP thermodynamic stability: energy above the convex hull of formation energies
entries.units: eV/ATOM (summary.py:172-175 'The energy above the hull in eV/Atom.'). is_stable is boolean (on-hull). AtomisticSkills' own mat-stability skill reports meV/atom after *1000 (pymatgen scan).
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.summary.search(fields=['energy_above_hull','is_stable']) -> SummaryDoc; mpr.materials.thermo.search(additional_criteria={'is_stable': True, 'thermo_types': [thermo_type]}) (mat-stability); filterable via criteria['energy_above_hull'] = (None, e_above_hull_max)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV/atom. Matches the map's IN-FLIGHT EnergyAboveHull candidate: registry tag 'energy_above_hull' = 'Per-atom distance above the convex hull of formation energies; zero means thermodynamically stable.' MP GROUNDS THIS DIRECTLY.

source: emmet/core/summary.py:172-175 (energy_above_hull eV/Atom), :177-180 (is_stable), :483-484 (thermo field list)

maps_to.notes: DEEP-REVIEW STATUS FLIP (new-node-candidate -> already-mapped): the EnergyAboveHull node IS present in docs/data/graph.json, and a real committed instance uses it: docs/data/instances/li2o-mp-1960-energyabovehull-atomisticskills-mat-phase-diagram-li2o.json (value 0.0 eV/atom, conditions {thermo: GGA+U, polymorph: ground state, hull: 'Li-O chemical system (Materials Project)'}). The registry has the 'energy_above_hull' tag. MP grounds it DIRECTLY (served field, eV/atom). Provenance is a SET of competing-phase energies (the whole hull), so the record carries the thermo_type / functional of the ENTIRE hull, not one material; conditions must record thermo_type. NOTE the real record uses conditions key 'thermo' with value 'GGA+U' (not the enum literal 'GGA_GGA+U'); see instance_source_design review note.

entries.name: mp-bulk-modulus
entries.quantity: MP bulk modulus, Voigt/Reuss/Voigt-Reuss-Hill averages of the DFT elastic tensor
entries.units: GPa. CRITICAL: MP serves bulk_modulus as a NESTED DICT {voigt, reuss, vrh} in GPa (elasticity.py:45-51 'Bulk modulus Voigt/Reuss/Voigt-Reuss-Hill average (GPa)'). This is ALREADY GPa, UNLIKE raw pymatgen ElasticTensor.k_vrh which is eV/A^3. NO 160.2176634 factor applies to MP fields.
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.elasticity.search(k_vrh=(min,max)) -> ElasticityDoc.bulk_modulus (BulkModulus model: .voigt/.reuss/.vrh); also SummaryDoc.bulk_modulus (dict). AtomisticSkills reads doc.bulk_modulus.vrh (get_elasticity.py:105-107).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: GPa is the map's BulkModulus canonical unit. NO conversion (unlike the pymatgen MLIP route which multiplies eV/A^3 by 160.2176634). The MP value is pymatgen's post-processed k_vrh already CONVERTED to GPa by emmet (elasticity.py:616-618 rounds k_voigt/k_reuss/k_vrh; the ElasticityDoc pipeline assumes the elastic tensor is in GPa, elasticity.py:248,316).

usage: mat-db-mp/scripts/get_elasticity.py:77-82 (k_vrh filter tuple, GPa), :102-107 (doc.bulk_modulus.vrh), :112 ('Bulk modulus (VRH): {bulk_vrh} GPa')
source: emmet/core/elasticity.py:45-51 (class BulkModulus: voigt/reuss/vrh in GPa), :322-325 (SummaryDoc.bulk_modulus dict GPa), :616-618 (k_voigt/k_reuss/k_vrh populated), :24-30 (elastic_tensor 'GPa')

maps_to.notes: Direct match to BulkModulus (GPa). TRAP CAUGHT: MP serves GPa directly (nested dict .vrh), whereas raw pymatgen ElasticTensor.k_vrh is eV/A^3 (pymatgen scan, needs *160.2176634). A consumer must NOT apply the 160x factor to MP fields. Declare the VRH averaging scheme + the DFT functional as provenance. Excellent instance source: (material, bulk_modulus.vrh, GPa) with conditions = {average: VRH, functional}. There is already a real instance record of this shape: docs/data/instances/cu-bulkmodulus-atomisticskills-mat-elasticity-cu.json (MLIP route); an MP record would be the DFT-database sibling.

entries.name: mp-shear-modulus
entries.quantity: MP shear modulus, Voigt/Reuss/Voigt-Reuss-Hill averages of the DFT elastic tensor
entries.units: GPa. NESTED DICT {voigt, reuss, vrh} in GPa (elasticity.py:53-58). Already GPa, no eV/A^3 factor (contrast raw pymatgen g_vrh).
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.elasticity.search(g_vrh=(min,max)) -> ElasticityDoc.shear_modulus (ShearModulus model); SummaryDoc.shear_modulus (dict). AtomisticSkills reads doc.shear_modulus.vrh (get_elasticity.py:108-110).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: GPa is the map's ShearModulus canonical unit. NO conversion (same GPa-not-eV/A^3 trap as bulk modulus).

usage: mat-db-mp/scripts/get_elasticity.py:84-89 (g_vrh filter tuple, GPa), :108-110 (doc.shear_modulus.vrh), :113 ('Shear modulus (VRH): {shear_vrh} GPa')
source: emmet/core/elasticity.py:53-58 (class ShearModulus voigt/reuss/vrh GPa), :327-330 (SummaryDoc.shear_modulus dict GPa), :621-623 (g_voigt/g_reuss/g_vrh populated)

maps_to.notes: Direct match to ShearModulus (GPa, .vrh). Same GPa-vs-eV/A^3 trap as bulk modulus. Instance source: (material, shear_modulus.vrh, GPa), conditions {average: VRH, functional}. Real sibling record exists: docs/data/instances/cu-shearmodulus-atomisticskills-mat-elasticity-cu.json (MLIP).

entries.name: mp-elastic-tensor
entries.quantity: MP full elastic (stiffness) tensor C_ij in structure and IEEE orientations
entries.units: GPa. elastic_tensor.raw / .ieee_format are Voigt 6x6 in GPa (elasticity.py:24-30 'Elastic tensor corresponding to structure/IEEE orientation (GPa)'). Compliance is served in 1/GPa*1000 = 1/TPa (elasticity.py:316-319 compliance*1000, 'assuming elastic tensor in units of GPa').
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.elasticity.search() -> ElasticityDoc.elastic_tensor (ElasticTensorDoc: raw, ieee_format); compliance_tensor also served (1/TPa)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: GPa is the map's ElasticConstants canonical unit. NO 160.2176634 factor (contrast raw pymatgen ElasticTensor.voigt in eV/A^3). Voigt packing is the reverse_voigt_map [[0,5,4],[5,1,3],[4,3,2]] (tensors.py, pymatgen scan). MP's ieee_format applies a standardized orientation (a representation choice) distinct from raw (structure orientation).

usage: mat-db-mp/scripts/get_elasticity.py (elasticity endpoint; full doc.model_dump() saved, elastic_tensor included)
source: emmet/core/elasticity.py:24-30 (elastic_tensor raw/ieee GPa), :297-319 (populate raw/ieee_format/compliance)

maps_to.notes: Direct match to ElasticConstants (rank-4, GPa). TRAP: MP serves GPa (raw + ieee_format 6x6), NOT eV/A^3. Two Voigt-orientation representations (structure vs IEEE); declare which. NOT a natural instance-source scalar (it is a tensor); the scalar VRH moduli above are the instance-friendly summaries.

entries.name: mp-youngs-modulus-and-poisson
entries.quantity: MP Young's modulus, Poisson ratio, and universal elastic anisotropy
entries.units: TRAP: young_modulus is served in SI PASCAL, not GPa (elasticity.py:188-189 'Young's modulus (SI units)'; :706 comment 'young's modulus (note it is in Pa, not GPa)', and elasticity.py y_mod uses pymatgen's 9.0e9 factor). homogeneous_poisson dimensionless (elasticity.py:194, summary.py:334). universal_anisotropy dimensionless (elasticity.py:191, summary.py:332).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: young_modulus: MP serves Pa; the registry's youngs_modulus tag (proposed) is GPa in the AtomisticSkills mat-elasticity convention (pymatgen scan). CONVERT MP Pa -> GPa by /1e9. poisson and anisotropy are dimensionless. E_Y = 9KG/(3K+G), nu = (3K-2G)/(2(3K+G)) are the closed forms (registry youngs_modulus / poisson_ratio descriptions).

source: emmet/core/elasticity.py:188-189 (young_modulus 'SI units', alias youngs_modulus), :191-194 (universal_anisotropy, homogeneous_poisson), :625-627 (young_modulus/homogeneous_poisson/universal_anisotropy populated), :706-708 ('note it is in Pa, not GPa')
emmet/core/summary.py:332,334 (universal_anisotropy, homogeneous_poisson)

maps_to.quantity_tag: youngs_modulus / poisson_ratio; universal_anisotropy has no tag
maps_to.notes: DEEP-REVIEW STATUS FLIP (new-node-candidate -> already-mapped for the two moduli): YoungsModulus and PoissonRatio nodes ARE present in docs/data/graph.json, with real committed instances docs/data/instances/cu-youngsmodulus-atomisticskills-mat-elasticity-cu.json (138.11 GPa, conditions {from: 'VRH moduli', model: MACE-OMAT-0-small}) and cu-poissonratio-... . MP grounds them directly. UNIT TRAP (verified beyond docstring): MP young_modulus is SI Pa. Source proof: emmet elasticity.py:625 populates young_modulus from pymatgen prop_dict['y_mod']; pymatgen elastic.py:199-204 y_mod = 9.0e9 * k_vrh * g_vrh / (3*k_vrh + g_vrh) with k_vrh/g_vrh in GPa, so the 9.0e9 factor carries GPa->Pa; and elasticity.py:706 in-source comment 'young's modulus (note it is in Pa, not GPa)'. DIVIDE by 1e9 for the map's GPa convention. universal_anisotropy (A_U, dimensionless) is STILL a quantity the map lacks entirely (no tag, absent from graph.json) -> genuine new-node candidate if the elastic-anisotropy domain is ingested.

entries.name: mp-magnetic-moment
entries.quantity: MP per-site magnetic moments and total/normalized magnetization of the spin-polarized ground state

dimension.note: magnetic moment in Bohr magnetons mu_B; normalized variants mu_B/f.u. and mu_B/A^3

entries.units: PRECISION-DUTY TRAP (multiple normalizations): (1) magmoms = PER-SITE list in mu_B (magnetism.py:52-55). (2) total_magnetization = TOTAL mu_B, i.e. PER CELL, and it is abs()-ed so SIGN IS LOST (magnetism.py:57-59 'Total magnetization in μB', :81-82 abs()). (3) total_magnetization_normalized_formula_units = mu_B/f.u. (magnetism.py:67-69). (4) total_magnetization_normalized_vol = mu_B/A^3 (magnetism.py:62-64). The primary field total_magnetization is PER-CELL total, NOT per formula unit; the per-f.u. value is the SEPARATE _normalized_formula_units field.
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.magnetism.search(total_magnetization=(min,max), ordering=Ordering(...)) -> MagnetismDoc (.magmoms, .total_magnetization, .total_magnetization_normalized_formula_units, .total_magnetization_normalized_vol, .ordering, .num_magnetic_sites); SummaryDoc carries the same total_* fields
entries.conversion_to_canonical: The map's registry tag 'magnetic_moment' = 'Per-site magnetic moment ... in Bohr magnetons' -> matches MP magmoms (per-site mu_B) DIRECTLY. total_magnetization (per-cell mu_B) is a DIFFERENT normalization: it is a sum over sites, not the per-site node. If a per-cell or per-f.u. magnetization node is ever added, use the correctly-normalized MP field; do NOT feed per-cell total_magnetization to a per-site MagneticMoment node.

usage: mat-db-mp/scripts/get_magnetism.py:70,79-92 (Ordering enum; total_magnetization filter tuple mu_B), :105-113 (doc.ordering, doc.total_magnetization 'μB'), :44 (FM/AFM/FiM/NM ordering)
source: emmet/core/magnetism.py:52-55 (magmoms 'Magnetic moments for each site'; the mu_B unit is not literally in this field's description, it is implied by the sibling total_magnetization 'Total magnetization in μB' field :57-59), :57-59 (total_magnetization TOTAL mu_B), :62-69 (normalized_vol mu_B/Å³, normalized_formula_units mu_B/f.u.), :81-83 (total_magnetization = abs(total_magnetization) with in-source comment 'not necessarily == sum(magmoms)'), :84-88 (magmoms come from CollinearMagneticStructureAnalyzer with round_magmoms=True, so they are ANALYZER-rounded, not raw VASP magmoms), :90-103 (populate)
emmet/core/summary.py:278-291 (total_magnetization + two normalized variants), :268-276 (is_magnetic, ordering)

maps_to.notes: DEEP-REVIEW STATUS FLIP (new-node-candidate -> already-mapped): the per-site MagneticMoment node IS present in docs/data/graph.json, with a real committed instance docs/data/instances/fe-bcc-magneticmoment-atomisticskills-mat-magnetic-density-fe.json (2.15 mu_B, conditions {site: 'atom 0', functional: 'PBE (MPStaticSet)', cell: '2-atom bcc Fe2'}) confirming the node is PER-SITE and that conditions is free-form (carries a 'site' key). The registry tag 'magnetic_moment' is PER-SITE (mu_B). MP grounds it via magmoms (per-site list). NORMALIZATION TRAP (this scan's headline for magnetism, UNCHANGED and VERIFIED): total_magnetization is PER-CELL total mu_B and is abs()-ed at magnetism.py:81-83 (SIGN LOST) so ferrimagnetic/antiferromagnetic sign structure CANNOT be recovered from total_magnetization; it must come from the per-site magmoms list. total_magnetization is also NOT per-site and NOT per-f.u. (the per-f.u. value is total_magnetization_normalized_formula_units). ADDED CAVEAT: MP magmoms are CollinearMagneticStructureAnalyzer-rounded (round_magmoms=True), and total_magnetization is 'not necessarily == sum(magmoms)' (in-source comment), so the per-cell total and the per-site sum can disagree. Map PER-SITE magmoms to MagneticMoment; treat total/normalized magnetization as distinct (per-cell / per-f.u. / per-vol) quantities needing their own nodes if ingested. ordering (FM/AFM/FiM/NM) is a discrete label.

entries.name: mp-band-gap-and-electronic-structure
entries.quantity: MP electronic band gap, Fermi level, metal/direct-gap classification, and band-structure/DOS records
entries.units: eV (band_gap summary.py:209-211 'Band gap energy in eV'; efermi summary.py:224-226 'Fermi energy in eV'). is_metal / is_gap_direct boolean (summary.py:229-237). cbm/vbm in eV.
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.summary.search(fields=['band_gap','is_metal','efermi']) -> SummaryDoc; mpr.materials.electronic_structure_bandstructure.get_bandstructure_from_material_id / electronic_structure_dos.get_dos_from_material_id -> pymatgen BandStructure/DOS; bs.get_band_gap()['energy'], bs.is_metal(), bs.efermi (get_mp_electronic_structure.py:57-84)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: eV; NO map node. Electronic structure is below the map's thermal/mechanical DAG floor (analogous to the deferred Kohn-Sham wavefunctions). Same candidate the pymatgen + VASP scans flagged (band_gap eV); MP is the DFT-database retrieval producer.

source: emmet/core/summary.py:209-237 (band_gap, cbm, vbm, efermi, is_gap_direct, is_metal), :244-252 (bandstructure, dos), :497-502 (electronic_structure field list)

maps_to.notes: No electronic-structure node on the map, and band_gap is a quantity the map lacks ENTIRELY (no registry tag) -> a genuine new-node candidate, high-value (most-used MP electronic field across mat-db-mp, mat-electronic-structure). Same candidate the pymatgen + VASP scans propose; MP is the retrieval producer (served band_gap eV). Excellent instance source once the node lands: (material, band_gap, eV), conditions {functional, is_gap_direct, method}. band structure E(k) / DOS are hidden electronic intermediates.

entries.name: mp-phonon-bandstructure-dos-forceconstants
entries.quantity: MP phonon band structure, phonon DOS, and interatomic force constants, retrieved as DFPT/phonopy records
index_structure: (FC) atom a, atom b, cartesian i, j, lattice vector R
entries.units: phonon frequencies in LINEAR THz (pymatgen PhononBandStructure/PhononDos, MP DFPT reference; pymatgen scan). ph_force_constants in phonopy-convention eV/ANGSTROM^2 (pymatgen scan). phonon_method selects dfpt / phonopy / pheasy.
entries.conversion_to_canonical: frequency: the map's canonical Frequency unit is LINEAR THz (SETTLED in the pymatgen review); MP/pymatgen also store linear THz -> CONSISTENT, no 2pi correction. 1 THz = 33.3564095198152 cm^-1 to the INVERSE_CM adapter. ph_force_constants eV/A^2 matches ForceConstants[order=2] (phonopy convention); QE grounds the same node in Ry/bohr^2 (factor 48.58681221205054).

source: mp_api materials.phonon endpoint; pymatgen/phonon/{bandstructure,dos}.py (THz-based); pymatgen MontyEncoder serialization

maps_to.notes: MP is a RETRIEVAL representation of nodes already on the map (Frequency, PhononDOS, ForceConstants[order=2]; all present in graph.json). Same as the pymatgen scan's phonon + force-constants entries; here the producer is the MP phonon endpoint directly. NO 2pi trap (linear THz, settled). phonon_method (dfpt/phonopy/pheasy) is a scheme/provenance field. Frequency is per-mode indexed (q, nu), not a single scalar; the instance store holds scalars, so phonon fields enter as spectra/arrays via the representation layer, not as scalar docs/data/instances records (cf. si-frequency-qe.json which is a spectrum instance).

entries.name: mp-mass-density
entries.quantity: MP mass density of the crystal (a quantity the map lacks entirely)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: NO map node and NO registry tag: mass density is a quantity the map lacks ENTIRELY. It is derivable from CellVolume + AtomicMass + AtomCount (rho = sum(m_i)/V), so it is a contract candidate rather than a fundamental source node. Would need a density dimension {M:1,L:-3} on the map.

source: emmet/core/structure.py:67-68 (density g/cm^3)

maps_to.notes: Mass density (g/cm^3) is a quantity the map lacks entirely. Low priority: fully derivable from CellVolume, AtomicMass, AtomCount (rho = total_mass/V), so a contract-tier node, not a new source observable. Listed as a new-node-candidate only for completeness of MP field coverage.

entries.name: mp-surface-energy
entries.quantity: MP Wulff-weighted surface energy and surface anisotropy (served by MP, NOT queried by AtomisticSkills)
index_structure: (implicit) Miller facets aggregated by Wulff construction
entries.units: weighted_surface_energy in J/m^2 (summary.py:370-373); weighted_surface_energy_EV_PER_ANG2 in eV/A^2 (summary.py:365-368); weighted_work_function in eV; surface_anisotropy, shape_factor dimensionless.
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.summary.search(fields=['weighted_surface_energy','surface_anisotropy','shape_factor']) -> SummaryDoc; mpr.materials.surface_properties (endpoint). NOTE: AtomisticSkills does NOT query these MP fields; it computes surface energy in-skill via MLIP slabs (mat-surface-energy, pymatgen scan).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: NO map node (matches the pymatgen scan's surface-energy new-node-candidate). 1 eV/A^2 = 16.021766339999996 J/m^2 (CODATA). MP serves BOTH units directly (weighted_surface_energy J/m^2 and weighted_surface_energy_EV_PER_ANG2 eV/A^2).

usage: (none in AtomisticSkills: MP surface fields are available but the skills compute surface energy via MLIP; listed for MP field coverage)
source: emmet/core/summary.py:363-393 (weighted_surface_energy_EV_PER_ANG2 eV/A^2, weighted_surface_energy J/m^2, weighted_work_function eV, surface_anisotropy, shape_factor, has_reconstructed)

maps_to.notes: DEEP-REVIEW STATUS FLIP (new-node-candidate -> already-mapped): the SurfaceEnergy node IS present in docs/data/graph.json, with real committed per-facet instances docs/data/instances/cu-{100,110,111}-surfaceenergy-atomisticskills-mat-surface-energy-cu.json (MLIP slab route). The registry has the 'surface_energy' tag (J/m^2 per facet). MP serves a Wulff-WEIGHTED surface energy (J/m^2 field summary.py:370-373 AND eV/Å^2 field weighted_surface_energy_EV_PER_ANG2 summary.py:365-368) plus anisotropy/shape_factor. MP-HAS-IT-BUT-UNUSED: AtomisticSkills computes surface energy via MLIP slabs, not MP. IMPORTANT distinction: the committed instances are SINGLE-FACET (hkl) gammas; MP's weighted_surface_energy is the Wulff-WEIGHTED aggregate over all facets, NOT one (hkl) facet, so an MP record is a different (aggregate) quantity from the per-facet MLIP records and is not a direct EXPECTED_AGREE with any single cu-1XX-surfaceenergy record.

entries.name: mp-intercalation-voltage
entries.quantity: MP average intercalation (electrode) voltage from the insertion_electrodes endpoint (served by MP, NOT queried by AtomisticSkills)
entries.units: VOLTS (electrode.py:62-64 'The average voltage in V for a particular voltage step.'). Companion fields: capacity_grav mAh/g, capacity_vol mAh/cc, energy_grav Wh/kg, energy_vol Wh/l, working_ion (Element).
entries.producing_api: mpr.materials.insertion_electrodes.search(working_ion=...) -> InsertionElectrodeDoc.average_voltage (V). NOTE: AtomisticSkills does NOT query this; it computes voltage in-skill via MLIP full/empty/metal cell energies (mat-intercalation-voltage, pymatgen scan).
entries.conversion_to_canonical: V is the map's Voltage canonical unit. Matches the map's IN-FLIGHT Voltage candidate: registry tag 'voltage' = 'Average intercalation (open-circuit) voltage: the Nernst energy difference over the transferred charge.' eV/e = 1 V exactly. MP GROUNDS THIS DIRECTLY via average_voltage.

usage: (none in AtomisticSkills: the insertion_electrodes endpoint is available but mat-intercalation-voltage computes V via MLIP; listed for MP field coverage)
source: emmet/core/electrode.py:62-64 (average_voltage V), :66-80 (capacity_grav mAh/g, capacity_vol mAh/cc, energy_grav Wh/kg, energy_vol Wh/l), :209 (working_ion), :261,388-397 (InsertionElectrodeDoc populate)

maps_to.notes: DEEP-REVIEW UPGRADE: the scanner marked this already-mapped by the registry TAG but left node:null ('graph node in-flight'). The Voltage node IS present in docs/data/graph.json, with a real committed instance docs/data/instances/lifepo4-voltage-atomisticskills-mat-intercalation-voltage-lifepo4.json (3.2604 V, conditions {working_ion: Li, reference: 'vs Li/Li+', model: 'MACE-MH-1 matpes_r2scan'}, MLIP route). MP serves average_voltage (V) directly via insertion_electrodes (electrode.py:62-64 'average voltage in V'). MP-HAS-IT-BUT-UNUSED by AtomisticSkills (which computes voltage from MLIP energies). MP is thus a DFT-database instance source and an EXPECTED_AGREE partner for the committed MLIP LiFePO4 record: (material=electrode couple, average_voltage, V), conditions {working_ion, functional, voltage_step}.

entries.name: mp-dielectric-constants
entries.quantity: MP static dielectric constants (total, ionic, electronic) and refractive index

dimension.note: dielectric constants dimensionless (relative permittivity); refractive index dimensionless

entries.units: dimensionless (summary.py:338-356: e_total, e_ionic, e_electronic total/ionic/electronic dielectric constants; n refractive index). e_ij_max is the piezoelectric modulus.
entries.conversion_to_canonical: The map's DielectricTensor node is the ELECTRONIC clamped-ion eps_inf (dimensionless). MP's e_electronic is the electronic contribution (~ eps_inf scalar); e_total = e_electronic + e_ionic. Matches DielectricTensor for the electronic part (as a scalar/isotropic reduction of the tensor).

usage: (mat-db-mp SKILL.md lists dielectric among computed properties; primary AtomisticSkills dielectric path is VASP LEPSILON/LOPTICS, atomate2-vasp scan)
source: emmet/core/summary.py:338-361 (e_total, e_ionic, e_electronic, n refractive index, e_ij_max piezoelectric)

maps_to.notes: MP's e_electronic (electronic dielectric constant, dimensionless) is a scalar reduction of the map's DielectricTensor (clamped-ion eps_inf, present in graph.json). e_ionic (ionic contribution) and e_total are DISTINCT quantities (the map's node is electronic-only); do not conflate e_total with eps_inf. n (refractive index) = sqrt(e_electronic) roughly. The frequency-dependent dielectric FUNCTION is the separate VASP-scan new-node-candidate. As an instance source, e_electronic could ground a scalar isotropic DielectricTensor value with conditions {functional}.

entries.name: mp-material-provenance
entries.quantity: The MP material_id, task_ids, thermo_type/functional, and MP2020 correction scheme: the provenance layer, not a physical quantity

dimension.note: not a physical quantity; database provenance

entries.units: n/a. material_id string 'mp-<int>'. thermo_type in {GGA_GGA+U, GGA_GGA+U_R2SCAN, R2SCAN, UNKNOWN} (types/enums.py:154-160 ThermoType; DEEP-REVIEW FIX: the scanner listed only 3 members and cited :154-159, but the enum has a FOURTH member UNKNOWN='UNKNOWN' on line 160). MP2020 corrections toggle energy_per_atom (corrected) vs uncorrected_energy_per_atom (raw). theoretical bool; deprecated flag on materials.
entries.producing_api: SummaryDoc.material_id / .task_ids / .theoretical; ThermoDoc.thermo_type; MPRester materials.summary/thermo endpoints; ThermoType enum (emmet/core/types/enums.py)
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no unit. This is the DATABASE PROVENANCE layer, the analog of the VASP scan's atomate2-workflow-framework OperatorRepresentationSpec: the functional (thermo_type / XC), the MP2020 correction scheme, and the mp-id are the provenance every MP-served value carries. A record's identity as evidence depends on this provenance.

source: emmet/core/types/enums.py:154-160 (ThermoType: GGA_GGA+U, GGA_GGA+U_R2SCAN, R2SCAN, UNKNOWN; the scanner omitted the UNKNOWN member)
emmet/core/summary.py:149-153 (task_ids), :409-414 (theoretical)

maps_to.notes: This is the provenance every MP value carries into a docs/data/instances record: source.ref = 'materials-project' + mp-id, and conditions carrying the functional / mixing-scheme (thermo_type) and correction scheme (MP2020 corrected vs uncorrected). See the instance_source_design object. Analogous to the atomate2-workflow-framework representation-only entry in the VASP scan, but for a DATABASE rather than an engine: MP's 'operator' is 'look up a precomputed VASP result', so its representation is a retrieval provenance, not a discretization.

entries.name: mp-structure-similarity
entries.quantity: CrystalNN-fingerprint structural similarity search over MP (a representation/retrieval operation, not a physical quantity)

dimension.note: similarity metric (dimensionless); not a physical observable

entries.units: dimensionless fingerprint distance; StructureMatcher tolerances (pymatgen scan).
entries.producing_api: mat-db-mp/scripts/find_similar_structures.py (CrystalNN fingerprint over MP summary structures); pymatgen SpacegroupAnalyzer.get_space_group_info; StructureMatcher
entries.conversion_to_canonical: no physical node; a similarity/identity operation on the Structure node (same status as the pymatgen scan's structure-match-and-novelty entry).

source: pymatgen/analysis/structure_matcher.py; pymatgen CrystalNN

maps_to.notes: Similarity/identity operation on Structure (retrieval over the MP database), not a physical observable. Same verdict as the pymatgen scan's structure-match-and-novelty. Belongs in the Structure node's compare()/identity semantics, not a new node.

instance_source_design.headline: Materials Project is the map's canonical DATABASE instance source: every MP record is a (material, value) pair = one piece of simulation evidence. Unlike an engine (which produces a value when run), MP hands back a value that a VASP workflow already computed, so the instance is a RETRIEVAL of precomputed evidence. This section specifies exactly how an MP record enters docs/data/instances/.
instance_source_design.instance_schema_recap: One JSON file per record (docs/data/instances/README.md): {variable, material, conditions, value, units, uncertainty, source:{kind, ref, detail}}. variable must be an exact id from docs/data/graph.json; source.kind is 'simulation' (MP values are DFT) or 'measurement'. Existing real records confirm the shape: cu-bulkmodulus-atomisticskills-mat-elasticity-cu.json (BulkModulus, GPa, MLIP), si-totalenergy-qe.json (TotalEnergy, Ry, QE), and CRUCIALLY li2o-mp-1960-{formationenergy,energyabovehull}-... which are the only MP-provenance records in the store today.
instance_source_design.DEEP_REVIEW_SCHEMA_FIT: The scanner's source_ref_format and conditions recommendations below were written as a forward-looking proposal and DO NOT match the two real MP-provenance records already committed (li2o-mp-1960-formationenergy / -energyabovehull). Field-for-field reality: (1) mp-id is carried in the 'material' field as 'Li2O (mp-1960)', NOT in source.detail as the scanner recommends; (2) source.ref is the SKILL name 'atomisticskills-mat-phase-diagram-Li2O', NOT 'materials-project' (no committed record uses 'materials-project' as ref; grep confirms zero); (3) conditions uses key 'thermo' with value 'GGA+U', NOT key 'functional'/'thermo_type' with the enum literal 'GGA_GGA+U'. Those records were produced BY a skill that queried MP, so ref names the skill. A pure-MP direct-ingest record (MP as producer, no skill) could still justify ref 'materials-project'; that is an orchestrator decision, not settled. conditions IS free-form (confirmed: real records carry keys 'thermo', 'polymorph', 'references', 'hull', 'site', 'cell', 'from', 'reference', 'working_ion'), so thermo_type/functional/average/correction all fit as free-form keys. The per_field_mapping 'conditions' examples below are DESIGN SUGGESTIONS, not the implemented key names; align key names with the committed pattern ('thermo', 'references') at encode time.
instance_source_design.kind: simulation. MP values are DFT (VASP) results; kind is always 'simulation', never 'measurement'.
instance_source_design.source_ref_format: DEEP-REVIEW CORRECTION: the scanner's recommendation below conflicts with the implemented schema. IMPLEMENTED PATTERN (li2o-mp-1960-*): mp-id goes in the 'material' field ('Li2O (mp-1960)'); source.ref is the producing skill; source.detail is a physics/provenance note plus the on-disk example path. For a PURE-MP direct-ingest record (no skill in the loop) the scanner's ref='materials-project' with mp-id in detail is a REASONABLE but UNADOPTED alternative; the encode-stage schema owner must decide whether to (a) follow the committed convention and put the mp-id in 'material' with ref naming the ingestion path, or (b) mint ref='materials-project'. SCANNER PROPOSAL (kept for the pure-MP case, flagged as not-yet-adopted): source = {"kind": "simulation", "ref": "materials-project", "detail": "<mp-id> <endpoint> (<thermo_type>); MP2020-corrected|uncorrected"}; example detail 'mp-149 elasticity (R2SCAN)'. Either way, the mp-id is the primary provenance handle and the endpoint records which document served the value.
instance_source_design.conditions_carry_functional_and_scheme: conditions is where the MP calculation provenance lives, mirroring how si-totalenergy-qe.json carries {pseudo, ecutwfc, k_mesh, code} and cu-bulkmodulus carries {average: VRH, model}. For MP, conditions MUST carry: (1) functional / mixing-scheme = thermo_type, one of {GGA_GGA+U, GGA_GGA+U_R2SCAN, R2SCAN} (ThermoType enum, emmet/core/types/enums.py:154-159); (2) for energetics, the MP2020 correction state (corrected vs uncorrected, since energy_per_atom != uncorrected_energy_per_atom); (3) the averaging/orientation scheme where relevant (average: VRH for moduli). Example conditions: {"functional": "R2SCAN", "average": "VRH"} for a bulk modulus; {"functional": "GGA_GGA+U", "correction": "MP2020"} for a formation energy.

FormationEnergy.variable: FormationEnergy (once node lands; registry tag formation_energy)
FormationEnergy.note: MP-fitted elemental references + MP2020 corrections; distinct reference state from the MLIP mat-* route.

EnergyAboveHull.variable: EnergyAboveHull (once node lands; registry tag energy_above_hull)
EnergyAboveHull.note: provenance is the WHOLE hull (a set of phases), all at the same thermo_type.

BulkModulus.note: GPa DIRECTLY (no 160.2176634 factor). Real MLIP sibling record: cu-bulkmodulus-atomisticskills-mat-elasticity-cu.json.

YoungsModulus.variable: YoungsModulus (once node lands; registry tag youngs_modulus)
YoungsModulus.note: MP serves young_modulus in SI Pa; DIVIDE BY 1e9 for GPa.

PoissonRatio.variable: PoissonRatio (once node lands; registry tag poisson_ratio)

MagneticMoment.variable: MagneticMoment (once node lands; registry tag magnetic_moment, PER-SITE)
MagneticMoment.note: use PER-SITE magmoms, NOT the per-cell total_magnetization (which is abs()-ed, sign lost) and NOT total_magnetization_normalized_formula_units (mu_B/f.u.).

TotalEnergy.note: per-atom energy_per_atom does NOT map to the per-cell TotalEnergy node; e_wo_entrp is a different VASP variant than the VASP scan's e_0_energy.

Voltage.variable: Voltage (once node lands; registry tag voltage)
Voltage.note: MP-has-it-but-unused by AtomisticSkills; EXPECTED_AGREE with the MLIP mat-intercalation-voltage route.

instance_source_design.not_ingested_as_instances: Structure (not a scalar), phonon band structure / DOS / force constants (spectra/tensors, enter via the representation layer like si-frequency-qe.json rather than a scalar value), and full elastic/dielectric TENSORS. Scalar reductions (VRH moduli, band gap, formation energy, e_above_hull, average voltage, e_electronic) are the instance-friendly fields.
instance_source_design.uncertainty: MP does not serve a per-value stochastic uncertainty for these DFT fields; uncertainty is typically null for MP records (unlike the MLIP MD records, e.g. lgps-activationenergy which carries a fit uncertainty). Systematic functional error is captured by the thermo_type in conditions, not by the uncertainty field.
instance_source_design.expected_agree_partners: MP records are natural EXPECTED_AGREE partners for the AtomisticSkills MLIP records already in the store: MP BulkModulus (DFT, GPa) vs cu-bulkmodulus (MACE-OMAT MLIP, GPa); MP FormationEnergy/EnergyAboveHull (DFT) vs mat-stability MLIP; MP Voltage (DFT) vs mat-intercalation-voltage MLIP. The cross-source agreement check is exactly what makes MP valuable as an independent DFT-database witness alongside the MLIP and QE/VASP producers.

open_questions: [STALE STATUSES - DEEP-REVIEW HEADLINE] The scanner ran against an EARLIER graph snapshot and marked FormationEnergy, EnergyAboveHull, YoungsModulus, PoissonRatio, MagneticMoment, SurfaceEnergy as new-node-candidate and Voltage as node:null. ALL SEVEN are now PRESENT in docs/data/graph.json (66 nodes total) and SIX have committed instance records (li2o-mp-1960-formationenergy, li2o-mp-1960-energyabovehull, cu-youngsmodulus, cu-poissonratio, fe-bcc-magneticmoment, cu-{100,110,111}-surfaceenergy, lifepo4-voltage). These entries were reclassified to already-mapped in this review. STILL genuinely tagless/absent: band_gap (no registry tag, no node), universal_anisotropy (no tag, no node), density (no tag, derivable). Consistency note for the orchestrator: the sibling pymatgen scan (same 2026-07-09 date) still calls FormationEnergy/EnergyAboveHull/YoungsModulus/PoissonRatio/Voltage/SurfaceEnergy 'new-node-candidate' in its verdicts; that scan's snapshot predates the nodes landing. Reconcile the two scans' statuses.

[NORMALIZATION - magnetization, headline] MagnetismDoc/SummaryDoc.total_magnetization is TOTAL mu_B (per cell) and is abs()-ed (magnetism.py:81-82), so SIGN IS LOST; the per-formula-unit value is the SEPARATE field total_magnetization_normalized_formula_units (mu_B/f.u.), and per-volume is total_magnetization_normalized_vol (mu_B/A^3). The registry's magnetic_moment tag is PER-SITE. Map PER-SITE magmoms to a per-site MagneticMoment node; do NOT feed per-cell total_magnetization to it. Decide whether the map wants per-site, per-f.u., and/or per-cell magnetization as distinct nodes.

[UNIT - elasticity, headline] MP serves elastic moduli in GPa directly (bulk_modulus.vrh, shear_modulus.vrh, elastic_tensor raw/ieee 6x6), UNLIKE raw pymatgen ElasticTensor (eV/A^3). Do NOT apply the 160.2176634 factor to MP fields. But MP young_modulus is SI PASCAL (elasticity.py:706 'note it is in Pa, not GPa'); DIVIDE by 1e9 for GPa. Two different unit conventions inside one MP elasticity document.

[ENERGY VARIANT + CORRECTION] MP exposes THREE energy quantities: uncorrected_energy_per_atom (raw DFT eV/atom), energy_per_atom (MP2020-corrected eV/atom), and the trajectory e_wo_entrp (raw per-cell eV). e_wo_entrp is a DIFFERENT VASP variant than the VASP scan's e_0_energy (pymatgen final_energy). AtomisticSkills deliberately uses raw trajectory e_wo_entrp for MLIP training to avoid MP2020 corrections (query_mp.py:141-142). A cross-code EXPECTED_AGREE with a QE/VASP TotalEnergy must match the SAME energy variant AND the correction state.

[FUNCTIONAL / MIXING-SCHEME PROVENANCE] thermo_type is one of {GGA_GGA+U, GGA_GGA+U_R2SCAN, R2SCAN, UNKNOWN} (ThermoType enum, types/enums.py:154-160; UNKNOWN was omitted by the scanner). GGA_GGA+U_R2SCAN is a MIXING scheme (r2SCAN energies referenced onto the GGA/GGA+U hull). Every MP-as-instance record MUST carry the thermo_type in conditions; a GGA hull and an R2SCAN hull give different energy_above_hull for the same material. NOTE the committed li2o-mp-1960 records store this under conditions key 'thermo' with the human value 'GGA+U' (not the enum literal 'GGA_GGA+U'); pick one convention at encode time.

[MP-HAS-IT-BUT-UNUSED] MP serves several fields AtomisticSkills does NOT query but which ground in-flight/registry nodes: average_voltage (V, insertion_electrodes endpoint) grounds the registry voltage tag; weighted_surface_energy (J/m^2 AND eV/A^2) grounds the registry surface_energy tag; e_electronic (dimensionless) grounds DielectricTensor. These are ready DFT-database instance sources even though the skills compute the same quantities via MLIP. EXPECTED_AGREE partners for the MLIP routes.

[QUANTITIES THE MAP LACKS ENTIRELY] band_gap (eV) and universal_anisotropy (dimensionless) have NO registry tag: genuine new-node candidates if the electronic-structure / elastic-anisotropy domains are ingested. density (g/cm^3) also lacks a tag but is derivable (contract-tier). e_ionic / e_total (dielectric), refractive index n, and the electrode capacity/energy-density fields (mAh/g, Wh/kg, ...) are further MP quantities with no map analog.

[DENSITY_ATOMIC IMPL/DOC MISMATCH] emmet SummaryDoc.density_atomic is populated as volume/num_sites = A^3/atom (structure.py:157) but its description string says 'atoms per cm^3' (structure.py:74). If ever ingested, trust the implementation (A^3/atom), not the description.

[mp-id AS SOURCE.REF] DEEP-REVIEW CORRECTION: the scanner's claim that ref='materials-project' with mp-id in source.detail 'matches the existing qe / atomisticskills-* convention' is FALSE. The two committed MP-provenance records (li2o-mp-1960-formationenergy / -energyabovehull) put the mp-id in the 'material' field ('Li2O (mp-1960)') and use ref='atomisticskills-mat-phase-diagram-Li2O' (the SKILL). No committed record uses ref='materials-project'. The existing convention is therefore: mp-id in material, ref names the producer/skill, detail is a physics note + example path. The scanner's ref='materials-project' proposal is a plausible option for PURE-MP direct ingest but is NOT the adopted convention; the encode-stage instance-schema owner (Appendix C of docs/openmaterials.tex) must decide. This scan does not touch omai/ or docs/data.

[emmet-core version] mp-api 0.41.2 / emmet-core 0.85.1 (miniconda base). Field names/units read from these installed versions; a future emmet could rename or re-normalize (e.g. change a normalization default), so pin emmet-core when an encode relies on a specific field's units. All MPRester routes used by AtomisticSkills verified present in mp_api 0.41.2.
